avoidRestatingSummary.minimumNewInformationRule: Each topic.summary must add at least one distinct, article-supported detail not already stated in summary.oneLiner or summary.bullets when such detail exists in the article. If no additional detail exists for that angle, explicitly say the article does not provide more detail rather than inventing content.

angles.name: recap
angles.whenToUse: Use only if it adds article-specific details not already present in summary.oneLiner and summary.bullets (for example: where, when, how it unfolded, what was announced, or what was filed). Do not restate summary.whyItMatters.

angles.name: details
angles.whenToUse: Prefer when the article contains concrete specifics such as figures, dates, quotes, filings, policy text, research findings, or operational details.

angles.name: players
angles.whenToUse: Use when multiple actors are named or when attribution is central. Preserve attribution language (said, alleged, according to, reported).

angles.name: timeline
angles.whenToUse: Use when the article contains two or more time anchors (dates, 'last week', 'on Friday', 'since', 'after'). If the article is timeless or lacks chronology, explicitly say so.

angles.name: next
angles.whenToUse: Use only when the article explicitly states a next step, planned action, scheduled event, or decision point. If not stated, say the article does not specify next steps.

angles.name: uncertainty
angles.whenToUse: Use when the article itself signals uncertainty, disputes, missing information, or incomplete reporting. Do not speculate beyond what is flagged as unknown.

angles.name: debate
angles.whenToUse: Use only when the article presents at least two distinct positions or contradicting accounts. Maintain attribution and avoid adjudicating who is right unless the article states it.

angles.name: watchlist
angles.whenToUse: Use only when the article identifies specific indicators or upcoming events that would change the story. Otherwise, prefer 'uncertainty' or 'next'.

handlingShortOrTruncatedArticles.rule: If the article is short, breaking, or lacks support for an angle, still produce 5 topics but make the topic.summary explicitly state what the article does not say. Do not pad with generic background.

titleQualityRules: Title must be specific and stand alone without context.
Title must not contain filler phrases or any bannedTopicPhrases.
Title must not duplicate any other title (case-insensitive) or differ only by a stopword.
promptQualityRules: Prompt must be one sentence and ask about article-specific facts, claims, or stated next steps.
Prompt must not ask for general background or opinions not present in the article.
summaryQualityRules: Topic summary must be exactly 2 to 3 sentences.
Topic summary must not repeat summary.whyItMatters content.
Topic summary must preserve attribution and uncertainty exactly as presented in the article.
If the article lacks support, topic summary must explicitly say the article does not provide that detail, without adding speculation.

grounding.description: Penalise any statement not clearly supported by the article.

nonRedundancy.description: Penalise overlap with summary.oneLiner, summary.bullets, and especially summary.whyItMatters.

specificity.description: Reward concrete nouns, named entities, numbers, dates, and attributed claims actually present in the article.

clarity.description: Reward short, keyword-forward titles and clear prompts.

passFailGates: Reject any topic if its title matches fillerTitles or contains fillerPhrases.
Reject any topic if its title or prompt contains bannedTopicPhrases.
Reject any topic summary that asserts causal impact unless the article explicitly states it.
